headline: Historic Pittsburgh Newspaper Saved by Nonprofit
meta_description: The Pittsburgh Post-Gazette, one of America's oldest newspapers, has been rescued from bankruptcy and will continue operating under nonprofit ownership.
summary_short: After facing closure, the 240-year-old Pittsburgh Post-Gazette has been saved through sale to a nonprofit journalism institute.
body:
The Pittsburgh Gazette, first published 240 years ago, has endured wars, economic upheaval, and technological revolutions. Known today as the Pittsburgh Post-Gazette, this historic newspaper was recently facing the threat that has claimed many storied publications: bankruptcy and closure.

Instead of disappearing into history, the Post-Gazette found salvation through sale to a nonprofit journalism institute dedicated to preserving quality local news. The rescue ensures that this cornerstone of Pittsburgh's civic life will continue serving the community and informing public dialogue.

The move reflects growing recognition that newspapers are not merely businesses but essential public institutions. By transitioning to nonprofit ownership, the Post-Gazette joins a growing movement of communities working to preserve independent journalism at a time when local news organizations face unprecedented pressure.
